item.title: Marshal Point 10 - Sands Lane, Left to Lady
Marshal Point 12 - Lady Woods go straight
Marshal Point 19 - Don't go down there!
Marshal Point 20 - Yes, DO go down there
Planning
item.description: Plans are coming along well, all systems go.

item.title: Walking the route
item.description: I carried out a recce of the route with the family today. It showed me that this route is both great fun, but challenging in places…

item.title: Volunteers needed
item.description:
Become a volunteer

Love getting up early?

item.title: Contact Us
item.description:
Get in Touch

Have a question about the Hopton 10k? We’d love to hear from you.

Before You Contact Us

You might find your answer in our:

  • FAQ page - questions about entering, eligibility, and what’s included
  • Race Day page - parking, registration, route, and logistics

How to Reach Us

Email

info@hopton10k.run

We aim to respond within 48 hours. During race week, we may be a little slower!

Social Media

  • Facebook - @hopton10k
  • Twitter - @hopton10k

Common Enquiries

Entry transfers or refunds: Contact us at least 24 hours before race day and we’ll do our best to help.

item.title: Donate
item.description:
Make a donation

To support our school and all the parents and teachers who are taking part in the Hopton 10k, please visit our Just Giving page to donate. Your support is greatly appreciated.

Donate Now

item.title: Frequently Asked Questions
item.description:
Got questions about entering the Hopton 10k? Find answers below. For race day specific information (parking, check-in, route details), see our Race Day page.

About the Race

When is the race?

Sunday 27th September 2026 at 10am

The Hopton 10k takes place on the first Sunday in October (or late September) every year.

Where does the race start?

The race starts and finishes at Hopton Primary School, Woodend Road, Mirfield, WF14 8PR. From there, the route heads through Lower Hopton before entering the beautiful woodland trails.

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.description:
Privacy Policy

Last updated: May 2026

The Hopton 10k is organised by a volunteer committee in Hopton, Mirfield, West Yorkshire. We are committed to protecting the privacy of everyone who enters the race, visits our website, or contacts us. This policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and your rights.

For any privacy-related queries, contact us at info@hopton10k.run.

What Information We Collect

Race entries (via RaceBest)

When you enter the race through RaceBest, they collect personal information on our behalf, which may include:

item.title: Race Day Information
item.description:
Everything you need to know to make your race day run smoothly. For general questions about entering, see our FAQ page.

Key Times

Time Activity
8:45am Registration opens
9:50am Registration closes
10:00am Race starts
11:30am Course cut-off (marshals pack up)

Getting There

Location

The race starts and finishes at Hopton Primary School, Woodend Road, Mirfield, WF14 8PR.

Important: Enter the school grounds through the playground gate on Chadwick Lane. The main reception and front gates will not be open.

item.title: Race Photos
item.description:
Hopton 10k Photos

Relive the action from past races! Browse our photo galleries from each year of the Hopton 10k.

2025

Photos from the 2025 race.

View Album

item.title: Race Results
item.description:
Hopton 10k Results

Since our first race in 2019, the Hopton 10k has welcomed hundreds of runners to tackle our challenging course. Below you’ll find the winners from each year along with links to the full results.

Looking for your time? Full results are published on RaceBest shortly after each race finishes.

2025

Male Winner

Adam Thompson (Stockport Harriers & AC)
40:38

Female Winner

Ciara Ellis
47:14

View full 2025 results

item.title: Sponsorship Opportunities
item.description:
Partner with the Hopton 10k

Looking for a unique opportunity to connect with hundreds of active, community-minded people in West Yorkshire? The Hopton 10k offers excellent visibility for local and national businesses alike.

All sponsorship funds go directly to supporting Hopton Primary School. To discuss options, use the contact form below or email info@hopton10k.run.

Package 1: Platinum sponsor (£800) – 1 package available

In return for your support as platinum sponsor, Hopton 10k would provide:

item.title: Terms & Conditions
item.description:
Terms & Conditions

Last updated: May 2026

These terms and conditions apply to all participants, spectators, and volunteers at the Hopton 10k.

The Organisers

The Hopton 10k is organised by a volunteer committee based in Hopton, Mirfield, West Yorkshire. All proceeds go to Hopton Primary School. The race is held under a UK Athletics Race Licence (No. 27783) and is subject to UK Athletics Rules of Competition.

Entry

  1. Entry is via RaceBest. By entering, you agree to these terms and conditions and confirm that you are physically fit to participate.
  2. You must be 15 years of age or older on race day to enter.
  3. Entries close at midday on the Saturday before the race. No entries are accepted on race day.
  4. Entry fees are non-refundable except at the sole discretion of the organisers. Contact info@hopton10k.run at least 24 hours before race day to request a transfer or refund. Refunds are subject to a £2.50 administration charge.
  5. Race numbers are non-transferable. You must not give, sell, or lend your number to another person without prior agreement from the organisers.

Race Rules

  1. All participants must follow the instructions of race marshals and officials at all times.
  2. The race must be completed within the 90-minute cut-off (by 11:30am). Marshals will begin to stand down after this time, and runners still on the course do so at their own risk.
  3. Headphones are permitted but strongly discouraged on trail sections for your own safety. You must be able to hear marshals’ instructions.
  4. Dogs (other than medical assistance dogs), buggies, and wheeled vehicles are not permitted on the course, in accordance with UK Athletics rules.
  5. The organisers reserve the right to refuse or withdraw any entry without giving a reason.

Health & Safety

  1. You enter and participate at your own risk. The organisers, sponsors, and volunteers accept no responsibility for any injury, illness, or loss sustained during the event, except where caused by negligence on their part.
  2. The race is covered by public liability insurance through UK Athletics.
  3. You should not participate if you are unwell, injured, or if a medical professional has advised against strenuous exercise.
  4. The course includes uneven terrain, woodland trails, stiles, and public roads. You are responsible for your own safety and for wearing appropriate footwear (trail shoes are recommended).
  5. First aid provision is available on race day. By entering, you consent to receiving medical treatment from event medical staff if required.

Course & Event Changes

  1. The organisers reserve the right to alter the course, start time, or event format for safety or operational reasons.
  2. In the event of cancellation due to circumstances beyond the organisers’ control (severe weather, public health restrictions, etc.), the organisers will endeavour to offer deferred entry to a future event but are not obliged to issue refunds.

Intellectual Property

  1. By entering, you grant the organisers permission to use your name, likeness, photographs, and video footage taken during the event for promotional purposes, including on the Hopton 10k website, social media channels, and printed materials. See our Privacy Policy for more details.
  2. Race results (name, finish time, position, running club) will be published on the Hopton 10k website and via RaceBest.

General

  1. These terms are governed by the laws of England and Wales.
  2. The organisers reserve the right to update these terms at any time. The latest version will always be available on this page.
  3. If you have any questions about these terms, please contact us at info@hopton10k.run.

item.title: Volunteer with Us
item.description:
Be Part of the Team

The Hopton 10k wouldn’t happen without our amazing volunteers. We need around 40 people to run the race safely, and we’re proud to have the friendliest marshals and helpers around.

What’s in it for you? A free T-shirt, goody bag, plenty of tea and coffee, and the satisfaction of cheering runners home. It’s a great morning out!

Interested? Email us at info@hopton10k.run or fill in the contact form at the bottom of this page.
